sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Dementia: assessment, management and support for people living with dementia and their carers
sources.version: NICE NG97; reviewed 2025-10-24 with no update required
sources.population: Adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia and their carers.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial assessment, specialist assessment, ongoing care coordination, and support.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult memory and cognitive concern assessment and support; partial for undifferentiated memory symptoms and not directly validated for UAE referral, driving, or social-care pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.11 — initial and specialist assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.34–1.2.35 and 1.3.1–1.3.2 — monitoring, coordination, and support plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.6 and 1.11.1–1.11.8 — driving information and carer support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.
